title: Garlicky Fluffy Portuguese Rice Pilaf
description: A kid-friendly, garlicky Portuguese rice pilaf that stays fluffy and FODMAP-friendly, accented with a splash of sake for depth of flavor.

ingredients.name: onion
ingredients.notes: finely diced to keep it kid-friendly

instructions: Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear.
Heat the olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat; add the minced garlic and diced onion, sautéing until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the rice to the saucepan and stir to coat the grains with oil and aromatics; toast for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour in the sake, stir, and let it evaporate for about 1 minute, allowing the rice to absorb the flavor.
Add the chicken broth, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 18–20 minutes until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is fluffy.
Remove the saucepan from heat, fluff the rice with a fork, then stir in the chopped parsley and lemon zest. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Let the pilaf sit covered for 5 minutes to steam and set; then serve warm. Optional: pair with grilled chicken or steamed vegetables for a complete meal.
